title: use of ARG for the FROM statement
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**
Currently, it seems that the use of ARG to be used by the FROM statement in a dockerfile does not work.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Extract from Dockerfile
ARG IMAGE_FROM=latest
FROM IP:9037/ubuntu-16.04-lts:${IMAGE_FROM}
2.buildah bud --pull --tls-verify=false --authfile /opt/app-root/src/.docker/config.json --tag IP:9038/shlib/buildockertu:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT -f Dockerfile --build-arg IMAGE_VERSION=1 --build-arg PRODUCT_VERSION=1.7.6 --build-arg PRODUCT_SHA1=0b6ec9fe34b29e6adc4d8481630bf1f69cb04aa9 --build-arg HTTP_PROXY=http://PROXY:3128 --build-arg HTTPS_PROXY=http://PROXY:3128 --build-arg http_proxy=http://PROXY:3128 --build-arg https_proxy=http://PROXY:3128



**Describe the results you received:**
STEP 1: FROM IP:9037/ubuntu-16.04-ltst:${IMAGE_FROM}
error creating build container: no such image "IP:9037/ubuntu-16.04-lts:${IMAGE_FROM}" in registry: image not known

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:       0.16
Go Version:    go1.8.3
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    82529cd
Built:         Fri Apr  6 10:15:58 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64

```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**


**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux 6516862c7fc7 4.4.0-104-generic #127-Ubuntu SMP Mon Dec 11 12:16:42 UTC 2017 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"

```

comments.body: @olivierGATSNCF I was not even familiar with the ARG command?

```
Understand how ARG and FROM interact

FROM instructions support variables that are declared by any ARG instructions that occur before the first FROM.

ARG  CODE_VERSION=latest
FROM base:${CODE_VERSION}
CMD  /code/run-app

FROM extras:${CODE_VERSION}
CMD  /code/run-extras

An ARG declared before a FROM is outside of a build stage, so it can’t be used in any instruction after a FROM. To use the default value of an ARG declared before the first FROM use an ARG instruction without a value inside of a build stage:

ARG VERSION=latest
FROM busybox:$VERSION
ARG VERSION
RUN echo $VERSION > image_version
```
@TomSweeneyRedHat @pixdrift Does one of you guys want to take a look at this?  Probably need support in imagebuilder first.
On my list to peak at.

Now also tripped-up by the lack of support for ARG-before-FROM (using buildah V1.0). Any idea when this will be supported so I can avoid the misleading **error determining starting point for build: no FROM statement found** message?

For now I'm forced to use multiple Dockerfiles.

If we got support in imagebuilder we got get this right away.  I have asked them to look into it.
@nalind Any idea on how hard this would be?
Is there any news on if/when ARG-before-FROM will be supported?
@alanbchristie I've not heard any news on this, if anyone has @rhatdan would have.  He's enjoying a rare day off today, I suspect he'll weigh in tomorrow.
Ooops, didn't mean to close this.  Reopening.
This should be fixed in buildah 1.5

Edit: nevermind, my fault, the Docker file had a bad syntax.

~~@rhatdan It is not:~~
```
buildah --version
buildah version 1.11.4 (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.1-dev)
```
```
cat tests/bud/copy-from/Dockerfile.step2
ARG MY_BASE_IMAGE
FROM scratch
FROM ${MY_BASE_IMAGE} as my-base-image
COPY --from=my-base-image /tmp/1.txt /tmp/1.txt
```
